_provenance: Human codon-usage frequencies from Kazusa CodonW human dataset (Codon Usage Database, http://www.kazusa.or.jp/codon/). Frequencies expressed as fraction of total usage of each amino acid. Used to back-translate the UniProt Q96S37 protein sequence into a representative CDS nucleotide sequence with GC content matching natural human-CDS character (~55-58% GC, NOT the 71% GC of a most-frequent-codon back-translation).
_strategy: Deterministic seeded weighted sampling. analyze.py uses a fixed seed so the back-translated CDS is reproducible across runs. The codon distribution per AA below matches Kazusa human values (rounded to 2 decimals). NOTE: this is still NOT the actual NM_144585.3 mRNA sequence — UTRs are absent, the actual codon distribution in SLC22A12 mRNA may differ from human-genome average, and synonymous codon choices in real SLC22A12 may cluster differently. See README.md Limitations.
